operating_state: Total revenue increased 4.3% to $22.4 billion in Q2 2024, with six-month revenue reaching $43.8 billion up 3.3% driven by 5.2% operational growth offset by 1.9% negative currency impact. Innovative Medicine grew from oncology performance with DARZALEX and CARVYKTI expansion, while MedTech growth reflected cardiovascular segment expansion including Shockwave acquisition contribution. Worldwide income before tax decreased to $5.7 billion from $6.3 billion with margin compression to 25.6% from 29.3%.
cost_structure: Cost of products sold increased to 30.6% of sales from 30.0% reflecting unfavorable product mix and macroeconomic pressures, compressing gross margin to 69.4% from 70.0%. Research and development expenses decreased to 15.3% from 17.2% driven by lower milestone payments and portfolio prioritization, while selling and administrative expenses remained stable at 25.3% of sales. Operating expenses included Shockwave acquisition and integration costs plus in-process R&D impairment.
material_events: Completed Shockwave Medical acquisition for $13.1 billion in May 2024 funded through senior unsecured notes, plus additional acquisitions totaling $2.05 billion. Recorded $3.0 billion incremental talc litigation charge bringing total reserve to $10.6 billion present value as part of proposed comprehensive settlement covering 99.75% of pending lawsuits. Completed debt-to-equity exchange of remaining Kenvue stake and maintained quarterly dividend at $1.24 per share with $1.6 billion in share repurchases.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation created 1.9% negative impact on worldwide sales with international operations experiencing negative currency effects. China volume-based procurement pressured multiple MedTech franchises. Interest rate exposure managed through notional swaps with weighted average debt cost of 3.28% and derivative positions totaling $85.5 billion notional across foreign exchange and cross-currency instruments.
forward_outlook: Management expects no COVID-19 vaccine revenue for remainder of fiscal 2024 and STELARA biosimilar competition beginning January 2025 following litigation settlements. Pursuing comprehensive talc litigation resolution through proposed settlement with three-month solicitation period initiated. Continuing integration of recent acquisitions including Shockwave's intravascular lithotripsy technology and bispecific antibody platforms, with MedTech orthopaedics restructuring program completing by end of fiscal 2025.
uncertainty_profile: Talc litigation remains unresolved with approximately 62,370 plaintiffs despite proposed settlement framework, while opioid litigation continues with remaining cases and outstanding payments. Multiple patent infringement cases pending across key products with various generic manufacturers. Government investigations active including DOJ Civil Investigative Demands regarding intraocular lenses, FTC inquiries into contracting practices, and Foreign Corrupt Practices Act investigations related to Brazilian medical device operations.
